understanding.goal_understanding: Solve each level by getting every selectable piece onto the goal tiles marked with '+' so that all goal tiles are occupied at the same time; when the engine reports WIN for the final level, the whole game is completed.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is an active/selected piece that responds to actions. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN shifts the selected piece one grid-step in that direction, but the step appears to be in fixed increments (e.g., x: 9→12 and 21→18), and movement is prevented by walls/obstacles (no change when blocked). CLICK attempts to move the selected piece to the clicked coordinate, likely by sliding along an unobstructed path (e.g., [12,21]→[21,21] succeeded), but if the destination is unreachable or blocked by walls/obstacles, the piece does not move. No evidence of pushing other pieces was observed in these turns; failed clicks behaved like blocked-path/no-effect actions rather than pushing.

orient_history: MOVE_* attempts move the currently selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) and collisions with wall tiles block movement. Since the selected piece is fully surrounded, progression likely requires using CLICK to either select the other piece or place/teleport a piece onto the goal area.

CLICK selects a selectable piece under the cursor; movement actions move the currently selected piece by its 3x3 footprint unless blocked by walls/objects. Current selected piece appears immobile, so we should switch selection to the other piece to attempt movement toward the goal.

Hypothesis: MOVE actions can’t work from this pocket (fully walled). CLICK may be a special relocation/interaction mechanic (possibly teleport/swap) that can place the selected piece onto a clicked goal tile even through walls; previous failure may have been due to click location/selection state.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is active (the UI reported 'moved' before, but both selectables still exist). Since the currently selected piece is immobile, we should switch selection to the other piece and reassess its available moves/interaction with the goal.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is currently controlled (selection), which the log describes as the selected piece ‘moving’ to the clicked selectable’s position. To make progress, we need to select the other piece and inspect whether it has any unblocked movement toward the goal region.

New hypothesis (based only on observed outcomes): CLICK primarily selects a selectable when clicked (my earlier ‘moves’ were actually selection switching between the two identical pieces), and does not relocate pieces onto empty/goal tiles. Actual piece motion, if possible, must be via MOVE_* actions (even if the UI currently reports blocked).

CLICK on a selectable changes which piece is selected; movement/CLICK-to-move only applies to the currently selected piece. Since the current piece cannot move, we should switch to the other selectable and try to route it toward the goal area (and later reposition both as needed).

CLICK likely changes which selectable is controlled when clicking on a selectable’s position; movement (MOVE_*) then applies to the currently selected piece. To make progress, we should control the other selectable and see if it has any unblocked moves toward the goal area.

Clicks on a selectable seem to swap/teleport the selected piece to that selectable’s pocket; arrow moves only work when there is an adjacent empty pocket (currently none). To make progress toward the goal area, I likely need to switch to the other selectable and probe its available moves.

CLICK seems to move the selected piece to the clicked location when it is aligned (same row/column) and not blocked by intervening collision; MOVE_* only works when adjacent cell is free. We likely must CLICK out of the fully-blocked pocket at [9,21] to a less-blocked square, then use MOVE_* to approach the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICKing a selectable position switches control to that selectable (or moves the controlled piece onto it), letting us continue movement from a less-blocked location. Arrow moves shift by 3 pixels; some areas are walled off so we may need to operate the other piece to reach the goal.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): CLICK is not the primary way to solve this; movement actions may be required to change game state (e.g., shift the selected piece within its chamber, trigger a selection swap, or otherwise reveal that only certain movement actions progress). Currently, the selected piece may be immobilized, so I will probe with a MOVE to see if blocked movement changes selection or state.

CLICK on another selectable swaps the selected piece with the clicked selectable; CLICK on empty does nothing. Arrow moves attempt to move the currently selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) if not blocked by walls.

CLICK appears to change which selectable is controlled when clicking on another selectable's position; MOVE_* then moves the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels) when not blocked by walls. To reach the goal, we likely must switch to the other selectable (at [18,21]) that previously had at least some mobility.

Movement (MOVE_*) is impossible from the current pocket; progress likely requires CLICK to teleport/place the selected piece directly onto a target cell (including goal cells) regardless of intervening walls.

CLICK appears to change which selectable piece is controlled (selection switches to the piece at the clicked coordinates). Since the currently selected piece is immobile, we should switch to the other piece and try moving it toward the goal tiles.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* moves the selected piece by 1 cell (3 pixels) if not blocked. CLICK attempts to relocate the selected piece to the clicked position; if that position is occupied by the other selectable, it likely swaps their positions, which would let us effectively reposition both pieces over time. To make progress toward the goal, we should get the selected piece onto the other selectable’s spot and then try moving from there.

My prior click-teleport strategy is unreliable; based only on what’s observed, the selected piece is currently in a fully enclosed pocket where normal MOVE actions might still reveal a hidden rule (e.g., pushing, swapping, or breaking walls) despite the UI claiming it’s blocked.

CLICK appears to change which selectable is controlled (by clicking on the other selectable’s location), while MOVE_* moves the currently selected piece by one cell unless blocked. Since the currently selected piece is fully blocked, we should switch control to the other piece and probe for a route toward the goal region.

CLICK appears to switch which selectable is currently active (selection), while MOVE_* moves the currently selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) when not blocked. Since selectable_9_21 is immobile, we should select the other piece that previously had some mobility and try to navigate it toward the goal region.

CLICKing a square occupied by the other selectable appears to swap the two pieces’ positions (effectively letting us act with the other piece). Direct MOVE steps from [21,21] are impossible due to surrounding walls, so progress requires using CLICK swaps and then moving from a less-trapped location.

MOVE actions are grid-step moves but walls fully surround the selected piece. CLICK appears to switch control/position to an existing selectable when clicking its coordinates. Hypothesis: CLICKing the goal’s exact pixel origin may place/teleport the selected piece onto the goal (previous goal-click may have missed the required exact target).

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to reposition the currently selected piece to the clicked pixel coordinate if it is a valid placement (likely needs to be grid-aligned and non-colliding). Goal tiles should be valid placement targets, but previous failures may have been due to misalignment/edge of goal area; try a different grid-aligned coordinate clearly inside the goal’s + area.

New hypothesis (discarding prior click-based one): CLICK is not the primary mechanic for solving; instead, MOVE actions may cause a different kind of movement (e.g., sliding/stepping or interacting with hidden passages) even when immediate adjacency looks blocked, so we should probe with MOVE to discover any non-obvious behavior.

CLICK likely switches which selectable is controlled (it appeared like a 'move' earlier because the controlled/selected piece changed). To make progress, I should control the other selectable and try navigating it toward the goal region.

Hypothesis: CLICK moves the selected piece to the clicked pixel position only if it lies on the same row/column (here same y=21), possibly ignoring intermediate walls; clicks with different y failed. If I click inside the goal region on the same y=21, the piece may land on the goal.

Movement by arrows requires an unblocked adjacent cell; CLICK sometimes relocates the selected piece but may require clicking inside the actual goal object area (not just its edge/aligned-to-grid corner). Since normal movement is fully blocked, the only plausible progress is a correctly-targeted CLICK within the goal's bounds.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is controlled when clicking on an existing selectable; movement keys then attempt to move the currently selected piece by 3 pixels but are blocked by walls in the current pocket. To reach the goal, we may need to switch to the other selectable and try a different approach/positioning.

CLICK appears to switch which selectable is controlled (the 'player' becomes the clicked selectable), while MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels). Since the current piece cannot move, progress requires selecting the other piece and attempting movement/placement toward the goal.

Discarding prior hypothesis. New hypothesis based on observations: CLICK can relocate the selected piece only within some valid reachable/allowed space, and the goal area seems separated by walls from the currently accessible area. Since the selected piece is now fully MOVE-blocked, a MOVE action may either (a) fail but change selection/focus, or (b) reveal an interaction (like toggling which piece is controlled) that CLICK did not accomplish.

CLICK may allow direct relocation to the clicked position (or some constrained teleport), potentially bypassing normal MOVE blocking; previous CLICK to the goal failed from a different position, but with only 1 step left the highest-value attempt is still to try landing on the goal.

Movement seems grid-based in 3-pixel increments; CLICK attempts to relocate the selected piece to the clicked position if a clear path/valid placement exists. With 0 steps remaining, no further effective moves are likely possible, but a CLICK is the only remaining attempt to place a piece onto the goal.

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: Solve each level by getting every selectable piece onto the goal tiles marked with '+' so that all goal tiles are occupied at the same time; when the engine reports WIN for the final level, the whole game is completed.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: There is an active/selected piece that responds to actions. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN shifts the selected piece one grid-step in that direction, but the step appears to be in fixed increments (e.g., x: 9→12 and 21→18), and movement is prevented by walls/obstacles (no change when blocked). CLICK attempts to move the selected piece to the clicked coordinate, likely by sliding along an unobstructed path (e.g., [12,21]→[21,21] succeeded), but if the destination is unreachable or blocked by walls/obstacles, the piece does not move. No evidence of pushing other pieces was observed in these turns; failed clicks behaved like blocked-path/no-effect actions rather than pushing.
